Sections.Heading: ## Features
Sections.Content:
- 4* Istanbul hotel

- Welcome dinner included

- All guided tours, excursions & entrance fees included

- Lunch included on Day 3

- Cruise on Day 3 included

- Return UK airport coach transfers

- Direct Turkish Airlines flights from London to Istanbul

- Professional local guide for all transfers & included day excursions

- Executive coach throughout

- Audio headsets included on Days 2 to 4

Sections.Heading: ## What We Will See
Sections.Content:
- Guided tour of Istanbul

- Blue Mosque

- Topkapi Palace & the Fruit Room of Ahmet II

- Hagia Sophia

- Underground Cistern

- Dolmabahçe Palace

- Included lunch & Bosphorus cruise

- Church of St Saviour in Chora

- Süleymaniye Mosque

- Grand Bazaar

- Spice Bazaar

Sections.Heading: ## Accommodation
Sections.Content: We stay four nights at the 4* Barcelo Hotel (www.barcelo.com/en-gb/barcelo- istanbul/) at Istanbul. Located at Taksim Square, facilities at the hotel include a restaurant and bar. All rooms are en suite and feature television, hairdryer, and tea & coffee making facilities. A welcome set three course dinner without drinks is included at our hotel on the first evening, and the remaining three nights are on a bed and breakfast basis.

Sections.Heading: ## Extras To Your Tour
Sections.Content:
- Single room supplement £299

- Insurance (including Covid cover) £92

- Gratuities

- Dinners on Days 2, 3 & 4

- Lunches on Days 1, 2, 4 & 5

Flight details may be subject to change. Price based on twin share. Minimum numbers required. Normal booking conditions apply.

### Day 1

We meet at our designated pick up points and transfer by private coach to our allocated London airport for our direct scheduled Turkish Airlines flight to Istanbul. On arrival at Istanbul we are met by our guide who will accompany us on all transfers and included day excursions. We transfer in our private executive coach to the 4* Barcelo Hotel in Istanbul for a four night stay. A welcome dinner is included at our hotel this evening.

### Day 2

This morning we meet our guide for a tour of the old city. We start by heading to the Sultanahmet district in the heart of historic Old Istanbul and our first visit is to the Blue Mosque. The masterwork of the 17th century Ottoman architect Sedefkâr Mehmet Aga, with its six minarets and a great cascade of domes, the Mosque is a great feature of the Istanbul skyline. We continue to Topkapi Palace. Home of the Ottoman sultans for nearly 400 years and the heart of the vast Ottoman Empire, Topkapi is today a museum of the old Turkish way of life. During our visit to Topkapi Palace, we see the Fruit Room of Ahmet II, adorned with fruit and floral paintings. This afternoon we visit the Byzantine Church of Hagia Sophia, among the finest architectural works in the world. Its wide, flat dome was a daring engineering feat in the 6th century and architects still marvel at the building's many innovations. Our day concludes with a visit to the Underground Cistern which was built in the 6th century and supplied water throughout the city during the Byzantine and Ottoman times. Re-discovered in 1545, it was used to water the gardens of Topkapi Palace.

### Day 3

Dolmabahçe Palace is our destination this morning, built by Sultan Abdül Mecit in 1856. The extravagant opulence of the palace belies the fact that it was built at a time when the Ottoman Empire was in decline. We then enjoy an included fish lunch. This afternoon we embark on an included cruise on the Bosphorus. We board the boat at Sariyer, a charming fishing village where the Bosphorus meets the Black Sea, and enjoy a perfect vantage point to see the Sultan’s palaces, Rumeli Fortress, the mansions and modern villas forming the elegant part of the city.

### Day 4

This morning we visit the Church of St Saviour in Chora, dating back to the 11th century and home to a fine selection of Byzantine mosaics and frescoes. We continue to the Süleymaniye Mosque. One of the grandest and most beautiful of the Ottoman mosques, it crowns one of Istanbul's seven hills and dominates the Golden Horn providing a landmark for the entire city. Our guide then accompanies us to the Grand Bazaar of Istanbul. With 60 alleyways and 3,000 shops, it is the oldest and largest in the world. The bazaar was established by Mehmet II shortly after his conquest of the city in 1453. Our day concludes with a visit to the Spice Bazaar. The bazaar came to specialise in spices from the Orient, taking advantage of Istanbul’s site on the trade route between the East (where most spices are grown) and Europe.

### Day 5

After spending the morning at leisure in Istanbul, we return to Istanbul Airport for our direct scheduled Turkish Airlines back to our allocated London airport. We then continue by private coach back to our original pick up points.
Sections.Summary:
NB:- Please note we reserve the right to alter the above itinerary if required for operational
reasons. Given the historic nature of the places visited, a certain degree of walking will be involved, and this holiday may not be suitable for those with mobility problems.
